source_jobs.title: Business Operations
source_jobs.description:
Ostium is on a simple mission: make it possible for anyone with a digital wallet to trade stocks, commodities, currencies, and crypto with full transparency. No brokers, no freezes, no hidden spreads. We’re replacing the opaque, offshore brokerage model with a transparent, permissionless trading stack built onchain. Every trade, deposit, and withdrawal is verifiable through open, auditable code. We’ve raised $27.9M+ from General Catalyst, Jump, LocalGlobe, Susquehanna (SIG), GSR, Alliance DAO, Soma Capital, Balaji Srinivasan, Meltem Demirors, and others.


ABOUT THE ROLE.

You'll work directly with our COO and Strategic Initiatives lead across finance, exchange and market data vendors, fundraising, legal coordination, and institutional relationships.



WHAT YOU'LL DO.

Exchange and data vendor ops

 - Own our vendor relationships with some of the largest TradFi exchanges in the world, and market data providers

 - Run the licensing, contracts, renewals, coverage, and cost across all of them

Analysis and planning

 - Take strategic questions from the founders and come back with data and a recommendation

 - Run quarterly planning: set targets with leadership, track them, keep them current

Finance ops

 - Own financial operations across entities and jurisdictions

 - Craft and execute appropriate incentives to manage capital with partner entities

Fundraising and investor ops

 - Run the operational side of fundraising: data rooms, diligence requests, investor updates, closing mechanics, legal and documentation review

Legal, entity, and institutional ops

 - Work with outside counsel on contracts, entity admin, and compliance

 - Point of contact for funds, market makers, and institutional partners across verticals on diligence, docs, and onboarding


WHO YOU ARE.

 - 3+ years in investment banking, consulting, or a high-growth startup

 - Strong modeling and spreadsheet skills

 - Clear communicator with counsel, banks, accountants, and institutions

 - Comfortable owning problems end to end without a playbook

 - You use AI tools in your daily work

 - You love markets. Crypto and DeFi fluency is a plus, not a requirement


COMPENSATION.

$170,000 $250,000 base, depending on experience.

source_jobs.title: Backend/Infra Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Ostium is on a simple mission: make it possible for anyone with a digital wallet to trade stocks, commodities, currencies, and crypto with full transparency. No brokers, no freezes, no hidden spreads. We’re replacing the opaque, offshore brokerage model with a transparent, permissionless trading stack built onchain. Every trade, deposit, and withdrawal is verifiable through open, auditable code. We’ve raised $27.9M+ from General Catalyst, Jump, LocalGlobe, Susquehanna (SIG), GSR, Alliance DAO, Soma Capital, Balaji Srinivasan, Meltem Demirors, and others.


OVERVIEW

Ostium is looking for a senior-level infrastructure/data engineer to spearhead our ambitious efforts on the infrastructure side. This engineer will work on the following:

 - Migrating applications from a legacy infrastructure, to a robust, new infrastructure.

 - Using 100% IaC to build, maintain, deploy, and manage applications and services.

 - Decreasing latency, increasing visibility, and finding ways to give us an edge (real financial impact on the business).

 - Maintaining, improving, and optimizing a new data pipeline to handle massive scale effectively.

 - Leading the company on best practices for both infrastructure and data engineering.

 - Empower every single developer at Ostium to self-serve: deploy new applications, request new infrastructure, monitor and alert—you name it.

We are building a state-of-the-art infrastructure layer that will allow engineers to self-serve 90% of their infrastructure needs. This will allow you to focus on higher-level projects and help us achieve ambitious goals.

 


TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

 - Core infrastructure fundamentals (security, networking, access control, etc.)

 - Strong experience with Kubernetes and Kubernetes best practices

 - Hands-on experience with Terraform, ArgoCD, GitHub Actions, Docker, Dagster, Prometheus, and Grafana. Evangelist of Infrastructure as Code (IaC)

 - Experience writing backend applications in Golang

 - Experience with Postgres, Clickhouse




CHARACTERISTIC REQUIREMENTS

Security Minded — Always looking to decrease security attack vectors.

AI Power Users — Not a vibe coder, someone who leverages AI effectively, validates outputs, and builds guardrails.

Solution Oriented — Engineers will have requests and needs; it's our job to make sure they can move as fast as possible.

Prioritize and Act — Be a leader and take stock of what's needed, what will deliver the most value, and propose your own work and path forward.

Humble and Willing to Learn — Be willing to learn from quants, engineers, the ops team, and everyone at this company about everything. We have no room in this company for people stuck in their ways.

Winner — This is a team sport, and there is a very real scoreboard. We want people who don't care about scoring the most points. They only care about the team winning and will do whatever it takes.

Disagree and Commit — Your voice will be heard and so will all your proposals and plans. But once a decision has been made, whether you agree or not, we expect 100% effort in execution.




WHAT YOU WILL WORK ON

Infrastructure Migration — As mentioned, we have a brand new infrastructure that we need to migrate our services to. You will help with this initiative.

Improve the New Stack — Find holes and gaps, prioritize them, and fill them. Come with both a critical lens and your toolbox.

Stack Orchestrator — An in-house tool for testing and validation of the various parts of our stack locally. Make it mature so developers can run their application in 10 different ways locally and work effectively with multiple agents in parallel.

Latency — Decrease latency as much as we can for our critical path applications. This means both working with app teams + writing application code.

Reliability and Security — Constantly think like a hacker who wants to take advantage of our system and bring it down. Leveraging AI tools and our existing tools to coordinate contained attacks on our sy

source_jobs.title: Backend Engineer (Pricing, Entity)
source_jobs.description:
Ostium is on a simple mission: make it possible for anyone with a digital wallet to trade stocks, commodities, currencies, and crypto with full transparency. No brokers, no freezes, no hidden spreads. We’re replacing the opaque, offshore brokerage model with a transparent, permissionless trading stack built onchain. Every trade, deposit, and withdrawal is verifiable through open, auditable code. We’ve raised $27.9M+ from General Catalyst, Jump, LocalGlobe, Susquehanna (SIG), GSR, Alliance DAO, Soma Capital, Balaji Srinivasan, Meltem Demirors, and others.


OVERVIEW

Ostium is looking for a mid/senior-level backend engineer to work on ambitious goals and projects. This engineer will work on the following:

 - Build out our entity service — a new central configuration plane for all our assets, providers, model parameters, and much more.

 - Work on a new, low-latency, highly reliable pricing system.

 - Work with quants, infra, data, and backend engineers to build a centralized pricing plane for every part of our system (real dollar value).

We are looking to build an extremely dynamic pricing system that will give our users the best possible price at any given moment. This system will determine if we win. We need an extremely ambitious and optimistic engineer who wants to win and is willing to put 100% effort into it.




TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

 - Strong Golang engineer that understands how to leverage AI effectively in the development process

 - Deep expertise with schema design, evolution, and iteration

 - Financial knowledge, experience with trading systems, and understanding of financial concepts

 - Experience with Postgres, NATs, Red Panda, GRPC, APIs, WebSocket, and other core modern services

 - Strongly leverages AI in the development process, but with extreme care and validation criteria




CHARACTERISTIC REQUIREMENTS

Risk Averse — Always looking to minimize paths where things can go dangerously wrong.

AI Power Users — Not a vibe coder, someone who leverages AI effectively, validates outputs, and builds guardrails.

Solution Oriented — There will be flaws; things will not be perfect. You must be willing to reason, prioritize, and propose paths forward that align with our business goals.

Prioritize and Act — Be a leader and take stock of what's needed, what will deliver the most value, and propose your own work and path forward.

Humble and Willing to Learn — Be willing to learn from quants, engineers, the ops team, and everyone at this company about everything. We have no room in this company for people stuck in their ways.

Winner — This is a team sport, and there is a very real scoreboard. We want people who don't care about scoring the most points. They only care about the team winning and will do whatever it takes.

Disagree and Commit — Your voice will be heard and so will all your proposals and plans. But once a decision has been made, whether you agree or not, we expect 100% effort in execution.




WHAT YOU WILL WORK ON

Entity Service API — This will be our core configuration plane where we will manage assets, mappings, market hours, providers, smart contract parameters, algo parameters, and much more.

Entity Service UI — A UI for every user in this company to propose changes, review them, and leave comments. Strong ACL and user management.

Pricing System — Assist in our new pricing engine to allow us to dynamically price every incoming transaction. The better prices we can offer, the more liquidity we can attract.



Commitment to Equal Opportunity
Ostium is committed to creating a culture of inclusion and belonging, and we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We believe it is our collective responsibility to uphold these values and encourage candidates from all backgrounds to join us in our mission. All qualified applicants will be treated with respect and receive equal consideration for employment without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, n

source_jobs.title: Talent Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
Ostium is on a simple mission: make it possible for anyone with a digital wallet to trade stocks, commodities, currencies, and crypto with full transparency. No brokers, no freezes, no hidden spreads. We’re replacing the opaque, offshore brokerage model with a transparent, permissionless trading stack built onchain. Every trade, deposit, and withdrawal is verifiable through open, auditable code. We’ve raised $27.9M+ from General Catalyst, Jump, LocalGlobe, Susquehanna (SIG), GSR, Alliance DAO, Soma Capital, Balaji Srinivasan, Meltem Demirors, and others.


ABOUT THE ROLE.

Every hire so far has been run by the founding team. You're our first in-house talent hire. You'll own the machine that keeps hiring moving, working directly with our Strategic Initiatives Lead and COO.

The breadth is the point. At a fund, a coordinator supports one desk. Here you'll help hire quants, engineers, and controllers, and also operators, marketers, creatives, and counsel. The full cycle, across every function, from first touch to signed offer.

Do this well and the path is obvious: grow to lead our recruiting function alongside the founding team.



WHAT YOU'LL DO.

Run the hiring machine

 - Own scheduling end to end: screens, technical rounds, onsites, debriefs

 - Keep Ashby clean: pipelines, feedback, and data people can trust

 - Chase interviewer feedback so no candidate sits in limbo

 - Draft offers and run pre-onboarding so new hires land ready on day one

Own the candidate experience

 - You're the first impression of Ostium: fast responses, sharp logistics, every detail right

 - Keep candidates warm through long processes and help us close at the end

Own the pipeline

 - Own our relationships with search firms and external recruiters: briefs, feedback, accountability

 - Source candidates yourself: LinkedIn, referrals, communities, wherever the talent is

 - Work with hiring managers in every function to keep searches moving

 - Report pipeline health to leadership: where candidates stall, what converts, where to push

Build the function

 - Turn what works into process: structured loops, scorecards, templates

 - Track hiring data from day one; data drives every decision here, recruiting included

 - How we recruit is yours to shape


WHO YOU ARE.

 - 13 years in recruiting coordination or agency recruiting; you know what a great process feels like

 - You've lived in an ATS (Ashby, Greenhouse, Gem or similar) and keep it accurate without being asked

 - You've sourced before and know how to get a reply, or you're hungry to learn

 - Organized to a fault: names, time zones, follow-ups, nothing slips

 - A clear, fast communicator with candidates, founders, and external partners

 - High ownership: you spot the ball dropping and catch it before anyone asks

 - Comfortable when priorities shift; you re-sort and keep moving

 - You use AI tools to move faster

 - Curious about markets; you'll be hiring people who live them

Compensation.

Base salary: $110,000 - $170,000

notes: High-tech startup with transparent trading stack, raised $27.9M+ from prominent investors.
